text.title: API Authentication Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped api-authentication work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: B2C web session uses 7-day JWT stored in localStorage; XSS bug exfiltrates token; user account abused until expiry.
antipattern.root-cause: JWT picked for cross-service is reused for browser session without server-side controls.
antipattern.fix: Switch B2C session to opaque cookie with session-store; reserve JWT for cross-service. See rule jwt-not-for-b2c-session.

antipattern.symptom: User clicks logout; backend has no JWT denylist; stolen token remains valid for 24h.

root-cause.source: OWASP API Security Top 10 — Broken Authentication

antipattern.fix: Implement denylist + short TTL OR move to opaque-session. See rule revocation-path-required.

antipattern.symptom: Same API key sits in a vendor integration since 2021; key leaks in a public repo; cannot rotate without scheduled downtime.
antipattern.root-cause: No rotation cadence; no graceful overlap.
antipattern.fix: Set 90-day cadence + N+1 active keys window. See rule api-key-rotation-cadence.

antipattern.symptom: B2B partner forced to OAuth2 authorization-code-with-PKCE because that is what the team built for the web app; partner integration timeline doubles.
antipattern.fix: Pick per-audience: client-credentials for B2B partners; session for B2C; JWT for cross-service. See rule scheme-matches-audience.
